text:
You are working on beads issue flt-it8.27: "MCP feature/docs alignment" (P1 bug).

## Issue Details
Project docs and README present MCP as an available capability, but the /mcp endpoint is still a placeholder that returns 501. Align externally stated capabilities with the actual implementation, and prevent placeholder endpoints from being represented as delivered features.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/mcp-align"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"
if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"; else git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"; fi
cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.

## Instructions
1. Read CLAUDE.md, api/CLAUDE.md, and README.md
2. Find the /mcp endpoint implementation (likely in api/internal/router/ or api/internal/mcp/)
3. Determine what MCP actually implements vs what docs claim
4. Two valid approaches:
   a. If MCP code exists but isn't wired up properly — wire it up and update docs to match reality
   b. If MCP is truly a placeholder — update docs to mark it as "planned" / remove false claims
5. Check README, CLAUDE.md, product-overview.md for MCP references
6. Ensure docs accurately reflect the actual state of MCP support

## Quality Gate
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```

## Completion
When done, commit, push branch, and report back with summary and quality gate results.
